page: Microsoft Azure D48s_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dsv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 48 vCPUs mapped to 24 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8573C processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The processor is equipped with a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix operations. The server is configured with 192.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local storage or onboard GPUs, relying instead on external Premium Storage options.

In benchmark evaluations, the D48s_v6 demonstrates strong performance characteristics across memory and compute workloads. Memory bandwidth tests show strong throughput, reaching 253.52 GB/sec for large-block read operations and 104.76 GB/sec for write operations. The system excels in image processing, achieving a top-tier rating in Geekbench tests, and delivers strong performance in software compilation, ray tracing, and text processing. Cryptographic and compression tasks also perform well, with single-threaded gzip compression measured at 31.39 GB/sec and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression reaching 305.53 GB/sec.

For application-level workloads, the server shows high efficiency in database operations and web serving. It handles 66.67 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and delivers a static web serving throughput of 81.09 GB/sec. Additionally, the hardware-assisted matrix extensions enable strong performance in CPU-based machine learning tasks, with LLM inference for text generation on a small model reaching a top-tier speed of 349.67 tokens per second. The lack of local NVMe storage represents a tradeoff, requiring network-attached Premium Storage for disk-heavy tasks, but the balanced resource density makes it a cost-efficient choice for enterprise applications, database hosting, web serving, and moderate machine learning inference.
description: Microsoft Azure D48s_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dsv6 family, powered by a 48-vCPU Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture with 24 physical cores, it features 192.0 GB of RAM, translating to a balanced 4.0 GB per vCPU. The server lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, but supports Premium Storage. It delivers strong benchmark results, particularly in memory bandwidth, image processing, and database operations, alongside top-tier performance in small-model LLM text generation. Supported by Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets, this server is well-suited for enterprise web serving, database hosting, software compilation, and CPU-based machine learning workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48s_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 48 vCPUs, an Intel Xeon 8573C processor, and 192.0 GB of RAM, delivering strong performance for databases and web serving.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48s_v6 is a general-purpose VM with 48 vCPUs, 192.0 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, optimized for databases and web serving.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose server with advanced Intel processors, optimized for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, and CPU-based machine learning.
bullet_points: 48 vCPUs and 192.0 GB RAM providing a balanced 1:4 resource ratio
Intel Xeon 8573C processor with AVX-512 and AMX instruction sets
Strong memory bandwidth and top-tier image processing performance
High-throughput database operations and Redis performance
No local storage, requiring network-attached Premium Storage
Suitable for enterprise web serving, software compilation, and LLM inference
